output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <cstdint>

// Note: push() and pop() are private, but we can observe their behavior
// through allocate() and deallocate() operations
template<size_t element_count = 10>
void demonstrate_block_allocator_behavior() {
    // Create a block_allocator instance with the specified number of elements
    hexi::block_allocator<std::byte, element_count> allocator;
    
    // Demonstrate allocation operations (which internally use pop() to remove from free list)
    std::byte* block1 = allocator.allocate();
    std::byte* block2 = allocator.allocate();
    
    // Demonstrate deallocation operations (which internally use push() to add to free list)
    allocator.deallocate(block1);
    allocator.deallocate(block2);
    
    // Additional demonstration: Allocate all available blocks
    std::byte* blocks[element_count];
    for (size_t i = 0; i < element_count; ++i) {
        blocks[i] = allocator.allocate();
    }

#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<typename T>
std::size_t encode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, T value) {
    return writer.write_varint(value);
}

template<typename T>
T decode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    T value;
    std::size_t bytes_read;
    reader.read_varint(value, &bytes_read);
    return value;
}

template<typename T>
bool encode_decode_roundtrip(T original_value) {
    std::vector<std::uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer, hexi::init_empty_t{});

    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader reader(adaptor);

    std::size_t bytes_written = encode_varint(writer, original_value);
    T decoded_value = decode_varint(reader);

    return original_value == decoded_value;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    // Create a buffer to hold the serialized data
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Create a binary stream for writing to the buffer
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    
    // Serialize packet_id (uint32_t)
    stream << packet.packet_id;
    
    // Serialize tags vector using prefixed_varint adaptor
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.tags);
    
    // Serialize data string using prefixed_varint adaptor
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.data);
    
    // Return the serialized buffer
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    
    // Create a binary stream for reading from the buffer
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    stream >> packet.packet_id;
    
    // Deserialize tags vector using prefixed_varint adaptor
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.tags);
    
    // Deserialize data string using prefixed_varint adaptor
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.data);
    
    // Check if stream is in good state after deserialization
    if (!stream.good()) {
        // Handle error state - in practice, you might want to throw an exception
        // or return a default-constructed packet based on requirements
        return CompactPacket{};
    }
    
    return packet;
}
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Serialization (`serialize_compact_packet`):**
   - Creates a `std::vector<uint8_t>` buffer to store the serialized data
   - Uses `hexi::binary_stream` to write to the buffer
   - Directly serializes `packet_id` as a uint32_t
   - Uses `hexi::prefixed_varint` adaptor for both `tags` vector and `data` string
   - The `prefixed_varint` adaptor automatically handles varint encoding of the size followed by the actual data

2. **Deserialization (`deserialize_compact_packet`):**
   - Creates a `hexi::binary_stream` using the provided buffer span
   - Directly deserializes `packet_id` into the result struct
   - Uses `hexi::prefixed_varint` adaptor to read the size and contents of `tags` vector
   - Uses `hexi::prefixed_varint` adaptor to read the size and contents of `data` string
   - Checks the stream state to ensure successful deserialization

The `prefixed_varint` adaptor efficiently handles variable-length integer encoding for the size fields, ensuring compact serialization where smaller values use fewer bytes. This implementation follows the Hexi library conventions as shown in the reference materials.</answer>
